name: RABBIT-FILE-BUG-11
title: item-status.py set allows no-op transition with misleading history entry
description: cmd_set in item-status.py does NOT check whether the current status equals the new status before appending a history entry. Calling 'set --status open --reason x' on an already-open item appends a fresh opened entry to history and re-pushes the item.json, polluting the audit trail with bogus state transitions. cmd_update has a no-op guard (returns early when value unchanged) but cmd_set does not. Fix: when item['status'] == args.status, exit 0 with a 'No change' message and append no history entry.

history.note: Wave 3: BUG-11 cmd_set short-circuits no-op transitions. BUG-14 verified already satisfied (commit_item backfill push already wrapped in _commit_and_push_with_retry by PR #112); new tests lock the invariant.
